title: gothofreda selloana
intro: Gothofreda selloana, also known as the Selloa, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Caprifoliaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Gothofreda selloana include its ability to reduce inflammation, relieve coughs, and ease congestion, making it a valuable remedy for respiratory conditions. Gothofreda selloana is typically prepared for herbal use through infusions or decoctions, and its leaves and flowers can be used fresh or dried to create teas, infusions, or tinctures.
